item.title: Roche/Sarepta's Elevidys (Duchenne Gene Therapy)
item.description:
• Summary: Roche and Sarepta are initiating a new Phase 3 trial for Elevidys (delandistrogene moxeparvovec) in Europe after regulatory rejections. The gene therapy aims to treat Duchenne muscular dystrophy by delivering a micro-dystrophin gene to improve muscle function, with ongoing efforts to secure approval.
  • MoAs: Adeno-associated virus (AAV)-based gene therapy delivering a truncated dystrophin gene to compensate for mutations in the DMD gene.
  • Sources: Endpoints News, BioPharma Dive, Fierce Pharma, STAT News
